sglang.title: Support multi-thread model weight loading
sglang.author: xianzhiT
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Support multi-thread model weight loading when using the --load-format "auto", which can speedup weight load process for users with distributed storage backend or NVME ssd disk.
Tests have found that storing DeepSeek-R1-0528 model weights on a single NVME drive for cold start of services

sglang.title: [PD] NIXL: Register kv args in advance and cleanup finished requests
sglang.author: trevor-m
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
This PR sends kv and aux base ptrs only once at the beginning and retains them in the prefill kvmanager for each peer. Similar to #5738 except we use the nixl agent name instead of mooncake session id as the dict key.
All of the state in mv manager for each bootstrap room is now cleaned up after the

sglang.title: misc: Improvement to serving_chat.py and add more ut
sglang.author: CatherineSue
sglang.bodyExcerpt:

The current _process_messages returns a 6-element tuple. It is hard to manage and error-prone.

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
The goal of this PR is to add to the benchmark the possibility to model more realistic request traffic.
Already existing request traffic scenarios (unthrottled traffic, constant request rate, burstiness) are useful, but now, with this "ramp-up" feature, we can gradually increase the request

vllm.title: [BugFix] Fix multi-node offline data parallel
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The offline data parallel example examples/data_parallel.py was broken because there's no outside synchronization tied to the engines running state, so other processes would fail once the first one finishes its requests and exits.

vllm.title: use .dev for version comparison with pytorch nightly release
vllm.author: BoyuanFeng
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
#19587 tried to use 2.8.0.a for enabling standalone_compile in vllm x torch nightly CI tests.
There is an issue that local pytorch build gives 2.8.0.a, but pytorch nightly release gives 2.8.0.dev. To save build time the ci tests uses pytorch nightly release instead of local pytorch build. Since 2.8.

vllm.title: Move to a faster base64 implementation
vllm.author: h-avsha
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR switches the default python base64 to pybase64 which is an optimized implementation. For large payloads (images and embeddings), it shaves off tens of milliseconds per request.
(The plot compares only base64 performance, not the entire request. X axis is the image resolution).

I tested that

vllm.title: [Feat][CLI] enforce-include-usage
vllm.author: max-wittig
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Currently, when streaming the usage is always null. This prevents the usage of limits per user and is a bit unexpected as without using streaming, the usage is always returned.
This can be used, if vllm is between a router, such as vllm-router or litellm and serves many users and is important to det

vllm.title: [Kernels][Bugfix] Use torch op for all kernels in FusedMoE forward.  Add additional testing for cudagraphs.
vllm.author: bnellnm
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
In order to avoid any issues with activation chunking and torch.compile, I've moved the remainder of the kernel calls in the FusedMoE layer into the moe_forward custom op.  I've also added some additional testing for cudagraphs at the pytest level for triton, deep gemm and cutlass.
